description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a large brown crustacean, resembling a lobster or crayfish, with a segmented exoskeleton and a robust, elongated body. The cephalothorax is broad and armored, tapering into a segmented abdomen that ends in a fan-shaped tail. It features two prominent claws, one larger than the other, and several pairs of articulated walking legs along its sides. The head is equipped with long, thin antennae and small, dark eyes positioned near the base of the antennae. The surface texture appears rough and matte, with a natural coloration of earthy browns and subtle reddish highlights on the joints and claws. This crustacean would be commonly found in seafood markets, restaurant displays, or as a decorative motif in urban food-related settings.
description: A large, brown, segmented crustacean with prominent claws, long antennae, and a rough, matte exoskeleton. Its body is detailed with articulated legs and a fan-shaped tail.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the fan-shaped tail and the symmetrical segmentation of the abdomen.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the large claw, multiple walking legs, and the full length of the antennae.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the broad cephalothorax, segmented abdomen, and the overall symmetry of the body.
description_view_3: Right side view emphasizing the claws, walking legs, and the curvature of the body and tail.
